title: Atelophragma aboriginum
intro: Atelophragma aboriginum, also known as Western American Atelophragma or Western Atelophragma, is a species of flowering plant in the family Aristolochiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of infections and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Atelophragma aboriginum include aristolochic acids, which are known to have cytotoxic and anti-inflammatory effects. Atelophragma aboriginum is often prepared as a tincture or decoction, and is used in herbal medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Atelophragma aboriginum is a species of frog within the family Bufonidae. It is a part of the genus Atelophragma. Atelophragma aboriginum is native to the southwestern United States and is a relatively small species of toad. The classification of this species is that of a toad, specifically a New World toad.
